verses.text: The wicked man has 'no fear of God before his eyes,' flattering himself, speaking deceit, plotting mischief even on his bed. Sin begins where the fear of God ends. A heart that drops God's awe slides into every kind of evil.

The wicked man has 'no fear of God before his eyes,' flattering himself, speaking deceit, plotting mischief even on his bed. Sin begins where the fear of God ends. A heart that drops God's awe slides into every kind of evil.

The wicked man has 'no fear of God before his eyes,' flattering himself, speaking deceit, plotting mischief even on his bed. Sin begins where the fear of God ends. A heart that drops God's awe slides into every kind of evil.

But God's mercy reaches to the heavens, His righteousness like great mountains, His judgments a great deep. His goodness is vast and unsearchable. Set against man's small wickedness, God's mercy fills the sky.

But God's mercy reaches to the heavens, His righteousness like great mountains, His judgments a great deep. His goodness is vast and unsearchable. Set against man's small wickedness, God's mercy fills the sky.

'How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God!' for men shelter under His wings, feast on His abundance, and drink from His river, for 'with thee is the fountain of life.' He satisfies the soul. All true life and light flow from God alone.

'How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God!' for men shelter under His wings, feast on His abundance, and drink from His river, for 'with thee is the fountain of life.' He satisfies the soul. All true life and light flow from God alone.

'How excellent is thy lovingkindness, O God!' for men shelter under His wings, feast on His abundance, and drink from His river, for 'with thee is the fountain of life.' He satisfies the soul. All true life and light flow from God alone.

David prays God's lovingkindness to continue to those who know Him, and sees the workers of iniquity already fallen. He contrasts the two ends. The mercy that upholds the godly outlasts the pride that topples the wicked.
